title: Fix accumulator.max bug in matrix pivot aggregation
description:
## Bug
In matrix/matrix.go lines 133-135, the max accumulator has a logic error. After the min block sets `a.minSet = true`, the condition `!a.minSet` on the max check is always false. This means `a.max` is never initialized to the first value if it's negative (since a.max starts at 0).

## Fix needed
Change the max condition to use a separate `maxSet` bool, or initialize max properly on the first call.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Matrix/MatrixObject.cs — accumulator logic

## Impact
AggregateFunctionMax returns incorrect results when all values are negative.

title: PreparedObject: add style fields for export rendering
description: PreparedObject in preview/prepared_pages.go needs additional fields for exports to render correctly: FontName string, FontSize float32, FontStyle style.FontStyle, TextColor color.RGBA, FillColor color.RGBA, HorzAlign int, VertAlign int, Border style.Border, ObjectType string (to distinguish text/picture/line/shape/checkbox). The engine should populate these from the source object when creating PreparedObjects. Without these, exports cannot style the rendered content.

title: End-to-end integration test: data → engine → HTML with text content
description: Create an integration test in reportpkg/ that: (1) builds a Report with a DataBand + TextObject containing [Field] expression, (2) binds a slice data source with 3 rows, (3) calls Report.Prepare(), (4) exports to HTML using html.Exporter, (5) asserts the HTML output contains the actual data values (not just band names). This is the critical smoke test that proves the full pipeline works: data binding → expression evaluation → PreparedBand.Objects → HTML rendering. This test will likely fail initially and drives the implementation of the other P0 tasks.

title: Add Report.Prepare() high-level method
description: Add a Prepare() error method to Report (reportpkg/report.go) that performs the complete report preparation flow: (1) initialize Dictionary data sources (call Init() on each), (2) evaluate expression-based parameters via Dictionary.EvaluateAll(), (3) create a ReportEngine, (4) call engine.Run() with DefaultRunOptions(), (5) store the resulting PreparedPages on the Report. Also add PreparedPages() accessor. This is the primary user-facing API for generating reports.

title: HTML export: render PreparedObject text and positions within bands
description: The HTML ExportBand in export/html/html.go currently renders only a band div with a name label. Update it to iterate PreparedBand.Objects and render each PreparedObject as a positioned HTML element: absolute-positioned div with left/top/width/height from the object, containing the evaluated Text content. Apply font styling (family, size, bold/italic), text alignment (HorzAlign/VertAlign), and border/fill from the object's style properties. This turns the HTML export from a band-skeleton view into actual report content.

title: PDF export: render PreparedObject text content in PDF output
description: The PDF ExportBand in export/pdf/export.go likely has the same skeleton-only rendering. Update it to iterate PreparedBand.Objects and for each text object: (1) calculate the PDF coordinate position (PDF uses bottom-left origin), (2) write text strings using the PDF content stream, (3) apply font name and size. The PDF core primitives (Contents, Writer) already exist — this wires them to actual rendered text from PreparedObjects.

title: Engine: wire data source row values into expression evaluator
description: When the engine iterates DataBand rows in RunDataBandFull (engine/databands.go), it must inject current-row column values into the expression evaluator before rendering band objects. For each row: (1) get all column names from the DataSource, (2) call GetValue for each column, (3) set them in the evaluator env so [ColumnName] expressions resolve. Also inject system variables (Row#, AbsRow#, PageNumber). This wiring connects the data layer to the expression layer so TextObject [Field] expressions produce actual values.

title: Engine: populate PreparedBand.Objects with rendered child objects
description: When the engine creates a PreparedBand in AddBandToPreparedPages (engine/bands.go), it only sets Name/Top/Height. It must also iterate the band's Objects() collection and for each child object (TextObject, PictureObject, LineObject, ShapeObject, CheckBoxObject) create a preview.PreparedObject with Left/Top/Width/Height and evaluated Text content. This requires calling Report.Calc() on TextObject.Text() to resolve [bracket] expressions. Implement in engine/bands.go in the showBand/AddBandToPreparedPages path.

title: Add Dictionary field to Report struct
description: Report struct in reportpkg/report.go has no Dictionary field. Add a *data.Dictionary field, a Dictionary() accessor, and initialize it in NewReport(). This is the foundation for expression evaluation — without it, the engine cannot access parameters, system variables, totals, or data sources at the Report level. Also add SetDictionary() for test injection.

title: Add Report.Calc() expression evaluator method
description: Add a Calc(expression string) (any, error) method to Report that evaluates bracket expressions like [DataSource.Field], [Parameter], [PageNumber]. Internally it should: (1) build an expr.Env from Dictionary parameters + system variables + totals, (2) convert [bracketed] tokens to bare identifiers, (3) call expr.Evaluator.Eval(). The engine will call this per-row to resolve TextObject text. Also add SetCalcContext(ds band.DataSource) to inject current-row column values into the evaluator environment.

title: Implement expression parser (bracket syntax)
description:
Port the expression parser for [DataSource.Field] bracket syntax.

Source: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Code/CodeUtils.cs -> expr/parser.go

Parse bracket expressions like:
- [DataSource.FieldName] -> data field lookup
- [ParameterName] -> parameter lookup
- [PageNumber], [TotalPages], [Date] -> system variables
- [Total.TotalName] -> total value
- [Price * Quantity] -> arithmetic
- [FirstName] + " " + [LastName] -> concatenation
- [IIF(Condition, TrueVal, FalseVal)] -> conditional

Parser must:
1. Find bracket pairs in text (configurable brackets, default "[,]")
2. Extract expression content
3. Resolve data references to actual values
4. Pass complex expressions to expr-lang/expr evaluator

TDD: Test parsing of all expression types, nested brackets, escaped brackets, invalid syntax. 100% coverage.

description:
Achieve 100% test coverage for functions/numtowords_in.go:

1. numtowords_in.go:90 in3digits (currently 25%) — CRITICAL: This function is at only 25% coverage. The Indian numbering system uses lakh (100,000) and crore (10,000,000) scales instead of the Western million/billion system. The function in3digits handles converting 3-digit groups but the lakh/crore scale logic is almost entirely untested.

Required tests:
- Numbers in the hundreds (100-999) to test basic 3-digit conversion
- Numbers in thousands (1,000-99,999) to test thousand scale
- Numbers in lakhs (1,00,000 - 99,99,999) to test lakh scale word generation
- Numbers in crores (1,00,00,000+) to test crore scale word generation
- Numbers in arab (1,00,00,00,000+) if supported
- Edge cases: exactly 1 lakh, exactly 1 crore, numbers with zeros in middle positions
- Verify correct Hindi/Indian English word output for each scale

title: Wire Table ManualBuild into engine rendering pipeline
description:
## Gap
table/helper.go implements ManualBuild with PrintRow/PrintColumn APIs and table/table.go:320-337 has IsManualBuild() and InvokeManualBuild() methods.

However, the engine NEVER calls InvokeManualBuild(). engine/objects.go:60 calls populateTableObjects() directly without checking IsManualBuild() first.

The ManualBuild feature is fully implemented but disconnected from engine execution.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Table/TableObject.cs (ManualBuild event)
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.Bands.cs (ManualBuild invocation)

## What needs to happen
1. In engine/objects.go populateBandObjects2() (around line 60), before populateTableObjects(), check if the TableObject has IsManualBuild() == true
2. If ManualBuild is set, call InvokeManualBuild() first to generate the result table
3. Then populate the resulting table's objects into PreparedBand
4. Write tests verifying ManualBuild callback is invoked during engine run
5. Test both row-priority and column-priority ManualBuild patterns

title: Bridge standard functions into expression evaluator
description:
## Gap
functions/standard.go exports All() with 50+ standard FastReport functions (math, string, date, formatting, type conversion, control flow) but these are NOT connected to the expr evaluator.

expr/functions.go (lines 26-38) only registers 8 basic functions: IIF, Format, DateDiff, Str, Int, Float, Len, Upper, Lower.

The comprehensive functions in functions/standard.go are never injected into the expression environment.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Functions/StdFunctions.cs
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Code/ExpressionDescriptor.cs

## What needs to happen
1. In expr/functions.go BuiltinFunctions(), merge functions.All() into the returned map
2. Or provide a RegisterStandardFunctions(env) helper that adds all standard functions
3. Ensure the engine's expression environment (engine/engine.go) automatically includes all standard functions
4. Verify name compatibility with FastReport expression syntax
5. Write tests confirming standard functions work in [bracket] expressions
6. Handle namespace collisions if any exist between the two function sets

title: Wire MSChartObject.RenderToImage into engine objects.go
description:
## Gap
MSChartObject now has a RenderToImage() method (added in object/mschart.go) using the chart/ package, but engine/objects.go still uses the old sparkline.DecodeChartData() fallback (lines 591-598).

Also, object/mschart.go:95 still says 'rendering is not yet implemented' — this comment is stale.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/MSChart/ (commercial feature)

## What needs to happen
1. In engine/objects.go, replace sparkline fallback with v.RenderToImage(w, h) call for MSChartObject case
2. Use the chart/ package renderer which supports Line, Bar, Area, Pie with multi-series
3. Remove stale 'not yet implemented' comment from object/mschart.go:95
4. Update engine/objects.go MSChartObject case (around line 591-598) to use RenderToImage
5. Write test confirming MSChart objects render properly through the engine pipeline

title: Engine CanGrow/CanShrink: implement text height calculation
description:
## Gap
Engine does not properly calculate text height for CanGrow/CanShrink bands. The BreakableComponent has these flags but text measurement is approximate.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/BreakableComponent.cs (CanGrow, CanShrink, CanBreak properties)

## What needs to happen

2. Use utils/textmeasure.go to measure text with word wrapping
3. Expand band height when CanGrow and text exceeds cell
4. Shrink band height when CanShrink and text is shorter
5. Handle CanBreak for page-spanning text
6. Write tests with multi-line text objects in CanGrow bands

No public API for binding Go data structures (slices, structs, maps) to report data sources at runtime.

title: PDF embedded images: implement picture object rendering in PDF
description:
## Gap
PDF exporter renders text, shapes, lines, borders, and checkboxes but does not embed actual image data for PictureObject. Images are stored in BlobStore as PNG/JPEG.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/Extras/Core/FastReport.OpenSource.Export.PdfSimple/PDFSimpleExport.Images.cs
- original-dotnet/Extras/Core/FastReport.OpenSource.Export.PdfSimple/PdfObjects/PdfImage.cs

## What needs to happen
1. Detect ObjectTypePicture with BlobIdx >= 0 in PDF ExportBand
2. Load image bytes from BlobStore
3. Detect format (PNG/JPEG) and create appropriate PDF image XObject
4. For JPEG: embed directly as DCTDecode stream
5. For PNG: decode, embed as FlateDecode RGB stream + separate alpha mask
6. Place image at correct position/size using cm transform
7. Write tests with PictureObject FRX files
close_reason: Already implemented: export/pdf/export.go:107-108 handles ObjectTypePicture with BlobIdx, renderPictureObject at :942 embeds images

title: PDF text wrapping: implement multi-line text rendering
description:
## Gap
export/pdf/export.go:192-201 — PDF text rendering only supports single-line placement. Text longer than the object width overflows PDF boundaries. No word wrapping, no multi-line support, no vertical alignment within bounds.

## What needs to happen
1. Implement word-wrapping logic in renderTextObject() that splits obj.Text into lines that fit within wPt
2. Use PDF text operators (TL for leading, T* for newline) to render multiple lines
3. Respect obj.HorzAlign (left/center/right/justify) for each line
4. Respect obj.VertAlign (top/center/bottom) for vertical positioning within bounds
5. Handle text clipping when content exceeds object height

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/Extras/OpenSource/FastReport.OpenSource.Export.PdfSimple/FastReport.OpenSource.Export.PdfSimple/PDFSimpleExport.cs — DrawTextObject method handles wrapping with font metrics
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Utils/TextRenderer.cs — text measurement and line breaking
close_reason: Implemented multi-line text wrapping with word-wrap, hard newlines, HorzAlign (left/center/right/justify with Tw), VertAlign (top/center/bottom), using PDF Tm operator for absolute line positioning

title: Deserialize Dictionary data sources from FRX (TableDataSource, connections)
description:
## Gap
reportpkg/loadsave.go:192-195 — the deserializeDictionary function hits a 'default' case that skips ALL data source children (TableDataSource, CsvDataConnection, JsonDataConnection, XmlDataConnection, MsSqlDataConnection, etc.). Column definitions, connection strings, SQL queries, and relation bindings from FRX are completely discarded.

## What needs to happen
1. Add cases in deserializeDictionary for 'TableDataSource': create a data.BaseDataSource, read Name, DataSource properties, iterate Column children
2. Add cases for 'CsvDataConnection': create data/csv source with connection string
3. Add cases for 'JsonDataConnection': create data/json source
4. Add cases for 'XmlDataConnection': create data/xml source
5. Store constructed data sources in the Dictionary
6. Handle 'Relation' children: read ParentDataSource, ChildDataSource, ParentColumns, ChildColumns

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Data/Dictionary.cs — Deserialize() method
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Data/TableDataSource.cs — data source with columns
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Data/DataConnectionBase.cs — connection base
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Data/CsvDataConnection.cs
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Data/JsonConnection/JsonDataSourceConnection.cs

title: Implement bandHeight CanGrow/CanShrink for singleton bands
description:
## Gap
engine/pages.go:157-168 — bandHeight() always returns the declared height, ignoring CanGrow/CanShrink. The comment says 'skeleton uses the declared height'. This affects PageHeader, PageFooter, ReportTitle, ReportSummary, and other singleton bands.

## What needs to happen
1. When CanGrow is true, measure text objects within the band using utils/textmeasure.go
2. Return the maximum of declared height and measured content height
3. When CanShrink is true, return the minimum of declared height and measured content height
4. Ensure the measured height accounts for text wrapping, font size, and object padding

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.Bands.cs — CalcHeight() method
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/BreakableComponent.cs — CanGrow, CanShrink properties
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Utils/TextRenderer.cs — text measurement

title: Implement BreakBand with object splitting between pages
description:
## Gap
engine/breaks.go:14-16 says 'simplified version'. When a band overflows, BreakBand splits only the height — it does not carry objects from the band's lower half. Child PreparedBands in the top/remainder portions are empty. Also engine/bands.go:167 starts a new page but does NOT call BreakBand for CanBreak=true bands.

## What needs to happen
1. When BreakBand splits a band, partition child objects between top and bottom halves based on their Y position relative to the split point
2. Objects spanning the split should be clipped or placed in the half where most of their area falls
3. Text objects crossing the split should ideally be split at line boundaries
4. The remainder band on the next page should contain objects from the lower portion

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.Break.cs — full break logic with object splitting
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/BreakableComponent.cs — Break() method implementation

title: Fix limitPreparedPages: trim pages slice to match capped count
description:
## Bug
In engine/engine.go lines 368-372, `limitPreparedPages()` updates `e.totalPages` but does not trim `e.preparedPages.pages` to match. The PreparedPages collection may contain more pages than the limit when MaxPages is set.

## Fix needed
After capping `e.totalPages`, remove excess pages from the PreparedPages slice. Add a `Truncate(n int)` method to PreparedPages if needed.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.cs — limitPreparedPages logic

title: HTML export: render Border properties on objects
description:
## Gap
export/html/html.go renderObject() handles FillColor (lines 129-135) but completely ignores PreparedObject.Border. All objects get default hardcoded borders instead of their actual border properties.

## What needs to happen
1. Read PreparedObject.Border (style.Border) and generate CSS border properties
2. Handle BorderLine.Color, BorderLine.Width, BorderLine.Style (Solid, Dash, Dot, DashDot, etc.)
3. Map each BorderLine.Style to CSS border-style values
4. Handle individual side borders (left, right, top, bottom can differ)
5. Handle Border.Shadow and Border.ShadowWidth as CSS box-shadow
6. Apply FillColor as CSS background-color (partially done, verify completeness)
7. Handle line objects with proper CSS rendering

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Export/Html/HTMLExportDraw.cs` — GetBorderStyle(), object rendering
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Border.cs` — BorderLines enum, LineStyle enum
close_reason: Added borderCSS helper in HTML exporter; TextObject border and fill now propagated from engine to PreparedObject and rendered as CSS border-* properties

title: PDF export: render Border, Fill, and proper font selection
description:
## Gap
export/pdf/export.go ExportBand() is minimal — it handles Text/Line/Shape/Picture but:
1. Border properties are ignored — renderRectObject() emits generic 're S' without checking Border.VisibleLines, Border.LineStyle, or Border.Width
2. Fill colors are never applied (no FillColor from PreparedObject)
3. All text uses hardcoded /F1 Helvetica regardless of Font.Name
4. No font embedding — only one built-in font is registered
5. CheckBox, Barcode objects are not handled

## What needs to happen
1. Read PreparedObject.Border and emit proper PDF path commands for each visible border side
2. Map LineStyle to PDF dash arrays (setlinedash operator)
3. Apply Border.Width via 'w' (setlinewidth) operator
4. Apply FillColor using 'rg' (setrgbcolor) operator before 'f' (fill) path commands
5. Register multiple PDF font resources (at minimum map common font families to PDF built-in fonts)
6. Select font by Font.Name matching to registered fonts
7. Add cases for CheckBox and Barcode objects

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/Extras/Core/FastReport.Data/FastReport.Export.PdfSimple/PdfObjects/PdfContents.cs` — PDF drawing commands
- `original-dotnet/Extras/Core/FastReport.Data/FastReport.Export.PdfSimple/PDFSimpleExport.cs` — Export logic
close_reason: PDF export now renders fill rectangles, per-side border lines with color/width/dash, drop shadows, and selects correct built-in Type1 font (Helvetica/Times/Courier variants) based on font name and bold/italic flags. Font resources pre-registered in page /Font dict.

title: Serialize Border and Fill on ReportComponentBase
description:
## Gap
report/reportcomponent.go Serialize() (lines 273-314) does NOT write Border or Fill properties. Deserialize() (lines 317-334) does NOT read them either. The border and fill fields exist (lines 63-64) but are never persisted to FRX.

## What needs to happen
1. In Serialize(), write Border properties (Border.Lines, Border.Color, Border.Width, Border.Style, individual side lines) using the delta-serialization pattern (compare against defaults)
2. In Serialize(), write Fill properties — for SolidFill write Fill.Color; for other fills serialize the fill type and sub-properties
3. In Deserialize(), read Border properties from dot-notation attributes (Border.Lines, Border.Color, etc.)
4. In Deserialize(), read Fill.Color for solid fills
5. Handle the existing style.Border struct which has Lines []BorderLine
6. Ensure round-trip fidelity: load → save → load should preserve borders and fills

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/ReportComponentBase.cs` — Serialize/Deserialize methods
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Border.cs` — Border serialization (Serialize/Deserialize)
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Fills.cs` — Fill serialization
close_reason: Implemented Border/Fill serialization and deserialization in ReportComponentBase. Added named color support and R,G,B format to ParseColor. Created borderfill_serial.go with full round-trip helpers.

title: Add Styles field to Report and wire StyleSheet to engine
description:
## Gap
Report struct (reportpkg/report.go) has no Styles field and no StyleSheet. The style.StyleSheet type exists but is not connected to the Report. The engine does not apply styles to objects during preparation.

## What needs to happen
1. Add a `styles *style.StyleSheet` field to Report struct
2. Add `Styles() *style.StyleSheet` accessor
3. Initialize styles in NewReport()
4. During engine Prepare, iterate all objects and call `styles.ApplyToObject(obj)` for objects that implement style.Styleable
5. ReportComponentBase already implements style.Styleable (has ApplyStyle and StyleName methods)
6. Wire this into engine/engine.go runPhase1 or runPhase2 before rendering

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Report.cs` — Report.Styles property (StyleCollection)
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Style.cs` — Style.Apply method
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/ReportComponentBase.cs` — ApplyStyle
close_reason: Styles field already existed in Report. Wired ApplyToObject call in engine/objects.go for each rendered object. title: Deserialize <Format> child elements on TextObject
description:
## Gap
TextObject.DeserializeChild() in object/text.go:499 only handles `<Highlight>` children. It does NOT handle `<Format>`, `<DateFormat>`, `<NumberFormat>`, `<CurrencyFormat>`, `<PercentFormat>`, `<BooleanFormat>`, `<CustomFormat>`, `<GeneralFormat>`, `<TimeFormat>` child elements.

## What needs to happen
1. Add a Format field to TextObjectBase or TextObject (e.g. `format format.Formatter`)
2. In DeserializeChild, add cases for each format child type
3. Deserialize format properties (DecimalDigits, DecimalSeparator, GroupSeparator, Pattern, etc.)
4. Create the appropriate format.Formatter and assign it
5. In engine/objects.go buildPreparedObject, after evaluating text, apply the format to the result value
6. Serialize the Format child back in TextObject.Serialize()

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Format/FormatBase.cs` — Base format class
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Format/NumberFormat.cs` — Number formatting
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Format/DateFormat.cs` — Date formatting
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Format/CurrencyFormat.cs` — Currency formatting
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/TextObject.cs` — Format property, Deserialize method around line 880
close_reason: Format field added to TextObjectBase with serialization/deserialization helpers; multi-format Formats child element handled in TextObject.DeserializeChild; engine applies format to single-expression text via evalTextWithFormat

title: Deserialize <Styles> element from FRX in loadsave.go
description:
## Gap
loadsave.go:88 falls through to `default: _ = rdr.SkipElement()` for the `<Styles>` child element. The StyleSheet struct exists in style/stylesheet.go but has no FRX deserialization logic.

## What needs to happen
1. Add a `case "Styles":` branch in loadsave.go `LoadFrom` child loop (line 78-92)
2. Implement `deserializeStyles(rdr, r.Styles())` that iterates `<Style>` children
3. For each `<Style>` child, read Name, Font, TextColor, FillColor, BorderColor and create a `style.StyleEntry`
4. Add the entry to the report's StyleSheet
5. Add serialization in SaveTo to write `<Styles>` back

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Style.cs` — Style class with Serialize/Deserialize
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/StyleCollection.cs` — StyleCollection
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Report.cs` — Report.Styles property
close_reason: Styles field already existed in Report. Wired ApplyToObject call in engine/objects.go for each rendered object. Implemented deserializeStyles/deserializeStyleEntry in loadsave.go with full attribute parsing. Added stylesSerializer/styleEntrySerializer for Save round-trip. All tests pass.

title: FRX smoke tests: Simple/Basic reports (7 files)
description:
Create test file reportpkg/frx_smoke_test.go (or a dedicated test-reports/ test package). For each .frx file, write a test that: (1) loads the FRX using serial.Reader, (2) verifies no parse error, (3) checks that at least 1 ReportPage was deserialized. Files to test:

- Text.frx
- Mail Merge.frx
- Unicode.frx

  func TestFRX_SimpleList(t *testing.T) {
    data, err := os.ReadFile("test-reports/Simple List.frx")
    require.NoError(t, err)
    r := serial.NewReader(bytes.NewReader(data))
    name, ok := r.ReadObjectHeader()
    assert name == "Report" and ok == true
    // verify no fatal errors during deserialization
  }

If no real font is available, use a simple character-width estimation (monospace approximation) as a fallback.

title: Engine: populate system variables during rendering
description: The engine must maintain and expose system variables throughout rendering so [PageNumber], [TotalPages], [Date], [Time], [Row#], [AbsRow#] resolve correctly. Add a *data.SystemVariables field to ReportEngine. Update: (1) startPage to increment PageNumber, (2) RunDataBandFull to update Row and AbsRow, (3) runPhase2 second pass to set TotalPages from first pass count, (4) Report.Calc to include system variables in the evaluator env. The data.SystemVariables struct already exists — this wires it into the engine lifecycle.

title: Implement CanGrow/CanShrink dynamic band height calculation
description: When a band has CanGrow=true, its height should expand to fit text content that exceeds the default height. When CanShrink=true, it should shrink when content is smaller. In engine/bands.go CalcBandHeight(), iterate the band's Objects() and for each TextObject: (1) measure the text using font metrics (golang.org/x/image/font), (2) account for word wrapping within the object's width, (3) if the text height exceeds the object height and CanGrow, expand. Update the PreparedBand height accordingly. This requires a simple text measurement utility in utils/ that calculates line count given text, font, and available width.
close_reason: Implemented CanGrow/CanShrink in CalcBandHeight. Measures TextObject content height using utils.MeasureText; expands band if CanGrow and content overflows, shrinks if CanShrink and content fits in less space. All tests pass.

title: Implement DataBand sort collection and row sorting
description: DataBand needs a Sort property (slice of sort.SortSpec with ColumnName and SortOrder=Asc/Desc). Before iterating rows in RunDataBandFull, if the DataBand has sort specs, the engine should sort the data source rows accordingly. Add: (1) SortSpec struct with Column string and Order SortOrder in band/types.go, (2) Sort() and SetSort() on DataBand, (3) sorting logic in engine/databands.go before the row iteration loop. For data sources backed by slices (BaseDataSource), sort the internal rows slice. For SQL data sources, the sort should be applied as ORDER BY.

Load must use the serial.Registry to instantiate the correct types for each XML element. The FRX reader/writer already exist — this wires them into the Report API.

title: Engine: implement column layout for DataBands
description: DataBand has Columns property for multi-column layout (e.g. 'Databand Columns, Row Numbers.frx'). When Columns > 1, the engine should arrange rows side-by-side in columns before advancing to the next row of columns. Currently column count is parsed but layout may use single column. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.Columns.cs.

title: Data: implement master-detail relation filtering in engine
description: When a report has Relation entries (e.g. EmployeesOrders linking Employees→Orders), detail bands should auto-filter to show only rows matching the current master row. engine/relations.go applyRelationFilters exists but may not handle all relation types. Verify with test-reports/Complex (Master-detail + Group).frx. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Data/Relation.cs.

title: FRX: parse and apply Style collections
description: FRX files define reusable styles (<Style Name='Header' Font='...' Fill='...'>) in the Styles section. Objects reference styles by name. Currently styles are loaded in style/ package but may not be applied during rendering. In engine/objects.go, resolve style references and merge properties. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/StyleCollection.cs, Report.Styles.

title: Image export: render TextObject rich formatting
description: Image exporter renders text with basic font but doesn't handle inline bold/italic/underline/color within a single TextObject (HTML-style tags). Parse <b>, <i>, <u>, <font> tags in text and render with appropriate font faces. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Utils/HtmlTextRenderer.cs.

title: Engine: implement CanGrow/CanShrink text auto-sizing
description: TextObjects with CanGrow=true should expand vertically to fit wrapped text. CanShrink=true should reduce height when text is shorter. The engine calculates required height in CalcBandHeight but the text measurement may not account for all formatting. Verify word-wrap height calculation matches C# behavior. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.Bands.cs CalcHeight.

title: PDF export: embed clickable hyperlinks
description: TextObjects with Hyperlink.Value set should produce clickable links in PDF output. Currently hyperlink data is serialized but not emitted as PDF Link annotations. In export/pdf/export.go renderTextObject, detect non-empty Hyperlink and add a /URI action annotation. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Export/Pdf/PDFExport.cs AddHyperlink.

title: HTML export: emit hyperlinks as <a> tags
description: TextObjects with Hyperlink.Value should render as clickable <a> tags in HTML output. In export/html/html.go, when rendering text objects, wrap content in <a href=...> if hyperlink is set. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Export/Html/HTMLExport.cs.

title: Implement Go expression scripting via callback hooks
description: C# reports use CSharp/VB.NET scripts for complex logic. A full script engine is impractical in Go, but we can provide a callback-based API: report.OnCustomCalc = func(name string, args []any) (any, error). Users register Go functions that the expression engine calls when it encounters [Script:FuncName(args)]. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Code/CsCodeHelper.cs. This is a pragmatic Go-idiomatic alternative to embedding a compiler.

title: Engine: implement Highlight conditions
description: TextObject and other components have Highlight property (ConditionCollection) that changes appearance based on expressions. The FRX loads HighlightCondition entries but the engine doesn't apply them during rendering. In showBand, evaluate each condition and apply Fill/Font/TextFill overrides. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.ShowBand.cs ApplyHighlight section.

title: Engine: wire up BeforePrint/AfterPrint event callbacks
description: Band OnBeforePrint/OnAfterPrint callbacks exist in band/band.go but the engine may not call them consistently during rendering. Verify all band types fire events in engine/bands.go showBand/ShowFullBand. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.ShowBand.cs fires BeforePrint/AfterPrint. Add tests verifying callbacks are invoked with correct sender.

title: Expression engine: add missing math functions
description: Port remaining math functions from C# StdFunctions. Go functions/ package has basics (Abs, Round, Floor, Ceil). Missing: Truncate, Sign, Log10, Exp, Pow, Sqrt, Sin, Cos, Tan, Asin, Acos, Atan, Atan2. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Functions/StdFunctions.cs. Register each in functions/standard.go.

title: Expression engine: add missing date/time functions
description: Port remaining date/time functions from C# StdFunctions. Missing: AddMonths, AddYears, AddHours, AddMinutes, AddSeconds, DaysInMonth, DayOfWeek, DayOfYear, IsLeapYear, DateDiff, DateSerial, TimeSerial. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Functions/StdFunctions.cs date section.

title: Expression engine: add missing string functions
description: Port remaining string functions from C# StdFunctions. Missing: PadLeft, PadRight, Trim, TrimStart, TrimEnd, Insert, Remove, IndexOf, LastIndexOf, Split, Join, IsNullOrEmpty, IsNullOrWhiteSpace, Concat. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Functions/StdFunctions.cs lines ~400-600.

title: MSChart: render pie/doughnut chart type
description: Implement pie and doughnut chart rendering for MSChartObject. Calculate slice angles from series values, draw arc segments with fill colors, add labels. C# reference: System.Windows.Forms.DataVisualization.Charting.

title: Export: RTF exporter
description: Implement RTF export format. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Export/RichText/RTFExport.cs. Should implement the Exporter interface with Export(pp, w) writing RTF format. Start with text objects, then add table/image support. RTF spec is simpler than DOCX — pure text-based format.

title: Export: CSV exporter
description: Implement CSV export format for tabular data extraction. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Export/Csv/CSVExport.cs. Should implement Exporter interface. Extract text from prepared pages, output as CSV rows. Support configurable delimiter, quoting, header row.

title: Register CSV data sources from FRX dictionary
description: FRX reports like 'Sales in the USA.frx' reference CSV data sources (e.g. DataSource='state_table_csv'). The data/csv package exists but the FRX dictionary deserializer doesn't auto-register CSV sources from CsvDataConnection entries in the FRX XML. The engine should parse <CsvDataConnection> or similar dictionary entries and create CsvDataSource instances. C# reference: original-dotnet/Extras/OpenSource/FastReport.OpenSource.Export.PdfSimple/CsvDataConnection.cs, CsvUtils.cs.

title: Support <inherited> root element for report inheritance
description: The FRX format supports report inheritance via an <inherited> root element instead of <Report>. Currently loadFromSerialReader in reportpkg/loadsave.go:129-132 rejects any root element other than 'Report'. C# reference: original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Report.cs (IsAncestor property, LoadBaseReport). The Go loader should detect <inherited>, load the base report first, then overlay inherited changes. Test: test-reports/Inherited Report.frx + Inherited Report - base.frx.

- min (chart.go:531) 66.7% / max (chart.go:538) 66.7%: the second branch (a>=b for min)
- globalRange (chart.go:500) 92.9%: single-point or constant series
- Render (chart.go:86) 96.7%: nil/empty series, zero-height
Write internal package tests (package chart) for unexported functions, and external tests via Chart.Render(). Verify: go test -coverprofile=c.out ./chart/... && go tool cover -func=c.out | grep -v 100

title: Coverage: web/handler.go — WithCORS 98% remaining branch
Add context.Context support to Report.Prepare and engine
description:
## Gap
Report.Prepare() in reportpkg/prepare.go has signature func (r *Report) Prepare() error with no context.Context parameter. The engine does not support cancellation or timeout.

The porting plan specifies: 'Use context.Context for cancellation' and 'accept context.Context as first parameter in long-running operations.'

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Report.Async.cs (CancellationToken support)

## What needs to happen
1. Add PrepareWithContext(ctx context.Context) error to Report
2. Pass context through to engine.New() and engine.Run()
3. In engine run loops (runReportPages, runDataBand, runGroup), check ctx.Done()
4. Return ctx.Err() when cancelled
5. Keep existing Prepare() as convenience wrapper using context.Background()
6. Add timeout support via context.WithTimeout
7. Write tests verifying cancellation stops report execution

title: Connect ProcessAt deferred text to TextObject evaluation
description:
## Gap
engine/processat.go has the deferred processing framework (EngineState enum, OnStateChanged, AddDeferredHandler) but there is no visible integration with TextObject's ProcessAt property.

TextObjects with ProcessAt=ReportFinished should defer their text evaluation until the report is complete, allowing totals and page counts to resolve.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/TextObject.cs (ProcessAt property)
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.ProcessAt.cs

## What needs to happen
1. Check if TextObject/TextObjectBase has a ProcessAt property in object/text.go
2. If ProcessAt != Default, register a deferred handler in engine during band rendering
3. The deferred handler should re-evaluate the text expression when the target state fires
4. Update the PreparedObject text in the prepared pages at deferred evaluation time
5. States: DataFinished, ReportFinished, PageFinished, ColumnFinished, GroupFinished
6. Write tests verifying deferred text shows correct totals after all data processed
close_reason: already implemented: engine/objects.go lines 72-92 registers deferred handlers, processat.go fires them at lifecycle events, pages.go/databands.go/groups.go call OnStateChanged

title: Render hyperlinks in HTML export
description:
## Gap
PreparedObject has HyperlinkKind and HyperlinkValue fields (preview/prepared_pages.go:272-274). PDF export renders hyperlinks (export/pdf/export.go:128-131, 545-619) but HTML export does NOT.

HTML export renderObject() never checks HyperlinkKind/HyperlinkValue.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Export/Html/HTMLExportDraw.cs (hyperlink <a> tags)

## What needs to happen
1. In export/html/html.go renderObject(), after rendering the object, check HyperlinkKind
2. For HyperlinkKind=1 (URL), wrap the object div in an <a href=...> tag
3. For HyperlinkKind=2 (PageNumber), generate an internal anchor link (#page-N)
4. For HyperlinkKind=3 (Bookmark), generate an anchor link (#bookmark-name)
5. Add bookmark anchors at the page level for bookmark targets
6. Write tests verifying <a> tags appear in HTML output for hyperlinked objects

title: Apply HighlightCondition during engine band rendering
description:
## Gap
style/highlight.go defines HighlightCondition struct with Expression, visibility flags, and style overrides (FillColor, TextFillColor, Font). However, the engine does NOT evaluate or apply highlight conditions during band rendering.

No code in engine/ references HighlightCondition.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/ReportComponentBase.cs (ApplyHighlight method)
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/HighlightCondition.cs

## What needs to happen
1. Check if ReportComponentBase has a Highlight/Conditions collection accessor
2. In engine/objects.go, before populating PreparedObject, evaluate each HighlightCondition.Expression
3. If expression is true, apply the condition's style overrides (fill, text color, font, border)
4. Apply in priority order (later conditions override earlier ones)
5. Ensure evaluated styles are reflected in the PreparedObject sent to exporters
6. Write tests with highlight conditions that change fill color based on data values

title: Render SVG objects inline in HTML export
description:
## Gap
export/html/html.go renderObject() has no case for preview.ObjectTypeSVG. SVG objects fall through to default case (line 402-404) and render as empty <div>.

SVG content is stored in BlobStore as UTF-8 bytes. HTML export should embed it inline as <svg> element.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Export/Html/HTMLExportDraw.cs (SVG handling)

## What needs to happen
1. Add case preview.ObjectTypeSVG in renderObject() in export/html/html.go
2. Read SVG bytes from BlobStore using obj.BlobIdx
3. Embed SVG content directly inside the positioned <div> as inline SVG
4. Sanitize SVG content (remove script tags for security)
5. Also add SVG placeholder rendering for PDF export (export/pdf/export.go)
6. Also add SVG placeholder rendering for image export (export/image/image.go)
7. Write tests verifying SVG embedding in HTML output

title: Preserve HTML markup in HtmlObject rendering
description:
## Gap
engine/objects.go:335-338 handles HtmlObject by setting po.Kind = ObjectTypeText and calling e.evalText() which strips HTML tags. The HTML markup is completely lost.

HtmlObject should preserve its HTML content so exporters can render it with formatting (HTML export embeds it directly, PDF/Image parse tags for styled text).

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/HtmlObject.cs
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Utils/HtmlTextRenderer.cs

## What needs to happen
1. In engine/objects.go HtmlObject case, evaluate bracket expressions within the HTML but preserve HTML tags
2. Either use a new ObjectType (ObjectTypeHtml) or store HTML text in po.Text with a flag
3. HTML exporter should output the HTML content directly (with sanitization)
4. PDF/Image exporters should use utils/htmltext.go to parse HTML tags and render styled text
5. Ensure expressions like [Field] inside HTML content are evaluated
6. Write tests verifying HTML formatting survives engine rendering

title: Add IdColumn/ParentIdColumn to DataBand for hierarchical reports
description:
## Gap
engine/engine.go:61-65 has hierarchyLevel and hierarchyRowNo infrastructure for hierarchical report state tracking. HierarchyLevel() accessor exists at engine.go:181-182.

However, DataBand in band/band.go has NO IdColumn or ParentIdColumn properties. There is no data binding mechanism to define which columns drive the hierarchy.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/DataBand.cs (IdColumn, ParentIdColumn properties)
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.DataBands.cs (RunHierarchy method)

## What needs to happen
1. Add IdColumn string and ParentIdColumn string to DataBand in band/band.go
2. Add FRX serialization/deserialization for these properties
3. In engine/databands.go, detect when IdColumn is set and switch to hierarchical iteration
4. Implement tree-building: group rows by ParentIdColumn, iterate in depth-first order
5. Set hierarchyLevel and hierarchyRowNo during hierarchical iteration
6. Support Indent property for nested indentation (pixels per level)
7. Write tests with a self-referencing data source (employee -> manager)
close_reason: already implemented: DataBand has IDColumn/ParentIDColumn fields+methods in band/types.go, engine/databands.go has runDataBandHierarchical

title: Implement table cell ColSpan support
description:
## Gap
matrix/header_tree.go:228 has a TODO comment: 'TODO: apply ColSpan = item.CellSize when table supports colspan.' Currently, colspan is worked around by filling span-width with empty cells.

The table rendering system (table/cell.go, table/table.go) does not apply ColSpan during layout.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Table/TableCell.cs (ColSpan, RowSpan properties)
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Table/TableResult.cs (span handling during layout)

## What needs to happen
1. Verify table/cell.go ColSpan() getter exists and is used during rendering
2. In table/result.go or table/helper.go, apply colspan during layout calculation
3. In matrix/header_tree.go:228, replace empty-cell workaround with actual ColSpan assignment
4. Update PDF exporter to respect ColSpan when rendering table cells
5. Update HTML exporter to emit colspan attribute on <td> elements
6. Write tests verifying multi-column spanning in matrix output

title: Implement MaxiCode Reed-Solomon error correction
description:
## Gap
barcode/maxicode.go:28 states: 'Reed-Solomon error correction is omitted — the RS codewords are filled with zeros. The symbol is therefore not scannable by a barcode reader, but it is visually accurate.'

This means MaxiCode barcodes generate correct visual patterns but cannot be decoded by scanners.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Barcode/BarcodeMaxiCode.cs (RS encoding logic)

## What needs to happen
1. Implement GF(64) Galois Field arithmetic for MaxiCode's RS coding
2. Implement RS encoder that generates error correction codewords
3. Replace the zero-fill at the RS codeword positions with actual computed values
4. Mode 2/3: Primary message (10 data + 10 ECC) + Secondary (84 data + 68 ECC)
5. Mode 4/5/6: Secondary only (93 data + 56 ECC)
6. Verify scannable output with a barcode reader/validator
7. Write tests comparing RS output against known reference values
close_reason: Implemented GF(64) Reed-Solomon encoder (maxiCodeRS). Replaced zero-fill with proper ECC: 10 primary + interleaved odd/even secondary ECC. All 144 codewords now include real error correction. Tests pass.

title: Expose password parameter in Report.Load/LoadFrom
description:
## Gap
serial/reader.go:72-82 implements NewReaderWithPassword() for transparent decryption, but reportpkg/loadsave.go Load() (line 24) and LoadFrom() (line 41) do not accept a password parameter.

Users cannot load password-protected FRX files via the Report API without dropping to the serial layer.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Report.cs (LoadFromString with password)
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Utils/FRReader.cs

## What needs to happen
1. Add LoadWithPassword(filename, password string) error method to Report
2. Add LoadFromWithPassword(r io.Reader, password string) error method to Report
3. These should use serial.NewReaderWithPassword internally
4. Keep existing Load/LoadFrom unchanged (backward compatible)
5. Add SaveWithPassword for encrypted output (using utils/crypto.go)
6. Write tests with encrypted FRX data

title: PDF text wrapping: fix multi-line text rendering accuracy
description:
## Gap
PDF exporter pdfWrapText/pdfEstimateTextWidth uses character-count approximation. Actual glyph widths needed for accurate wrapping.

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/Extras/Core/FastReport.OpenSource.Export.PdfSimple/PDFSimpleExport.cs

## What needs to happen
1. Load standard 14 PDF font metrics (widths per glyph)
2. Use actual glyph widths for text measurement
3. Improve word-wrap algorithm for multi-line text
4. Handle text alignment (left, center, right, justify)
5. Support vertical alignment (top, center, bottom)
6. Write visual comparison tests
close_reason: Text wrapping now uses sfnt glyph advance widths via fontMgr.MeasureText(), replacing the crude 0.6x heuristic. pdfWrapTextFn accepts a custom measureFn so embedded fonts use accurate metrics.

title: PDF watermark: use proper ExtGState for transparency
description:
## Gap
export/pdf/export.go:465 — Watermark transparency uses a hack: `Tr` (text rendering mode) set to alpha*2 instead of proper PDF transparency via ExtGState graphics state objects.

## What needs to happen
1. Create an ExtGState dictionary with /ca (fill opacity) and /CA (stroke opacity) keys
2. Register it as a page resource under /ExtGState
3. Use `/GSx gs` operator before drawing watermark text instead of `Tr` hack
4. Support transparency values from 0.0 (invisible) to 1.0 (opaque)

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/Extras/OpenSource/FastReport.OpenSource.Export.PdfSimple/FastReport.OpenSource.Export.PdfSimple/PDFSimpleExport.cs — watermark rendering with proper alpha blending
- PDF Reference 1.7 Section 11.6.4.4 — Transparency via ExtGState
close_reason: Replaced Tr transparency hack with proper ExtGState /ca /CA fill and stroke opacity. Added AddExtGState method to Page, lazy-creates /ExtGState resource dict

## C# Reference

- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Table/TableResult.cs

close_reason: Fixed FRX round-trip bugs: RepeatHeaders default true, MaxWidth 5000, MaxHeight 1000, CellDuplicates attribute name+enum string serialization; added ManualBuildAutoSpans to TableObject; TableCellData/Helper/Result/StyleCollection are runtime-only with no FRX impact

title: Implement PreparedPagePostprocessor for totals/page-number fixup
description:
## Gap
No Go equivalent of Preview/PreparedPagePostprocessor.cs. After the engine finishes, a post-processing pass is needed to:
1. Finalize [TotalPages] values in deferred text objects
2. Fix up page numbering for multi-page reports
3. Handle two-pass rendering adjustments

## What needs to happen
1. Create preview/postprocessor.go
2. After engine run completes, iterate all PreparedPages
3. Replace any remaining [TotalPages] placeholders with the actual page count
4. Handle other deferred system variable replacements
5. Call from reportpkg/prepare.go after engine.Run() completes

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Preview/PreparedPagePostprocessor.cs — post-processing logic
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Preview/PreparedPages.cs — PostprocessPage method
close_reason: Implemented PreparedPagePostprocessor in preview/postprocessor.go with TotalPages/PageCount/Page placeholder replacement across all PreparedPages bands and objects

title: Implement HtmlTextRenderer for rich inline HTML in text objects
description:
## Gap No Go equivalent of Utils/HtmlTextRenderer.cs. TextObjects with TextRenderType=HtmlTags contain inline HTML (, ,
, , etc.) that should be parsed and rendered with formatting. Currently the raw HTML tags appear as literal text in PDF and image exports. ## What needs to happen 1. Create utils/htmltext.go with an HtmlTextRenderer 2. Parse basic HTML tags: , , ,
, , , 3. Strip HTML tags for plain-text fallback (used by PDF/image exporters) 4. For HTML export: pass the content through as-is (already HTML) 5. For PDF export: extract text runs with formatting attributes 6. Update engine/objects.go to detect TextRenderType=HtmlTags and use the renderer ## C# Reference - original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Utils/HtmlTextRenderer.cs — full HTML text parser/renderer - original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/TextObject.cs — TextRenderType enum

title: Render SVGObject: decode SvgData and store as PNG blob
description:
## Gap
engine/objects.go:454-456 — SVGObject emits ObjectTypePicture with BlobIdx=-1 (no blob). The SvgData field is stored in the object but never decoded or placed in BlobStore. All three exporters check BlobIdx >= 0 before rendering images, so SVG objects always render as empty boxes.

## What needs to happen
1. In buildPreparedObject, for SVGObject: read v.SvgData (raw SVG XML string)
2. If SvgData contains embedded base64 image data, extract and decode it
3. Alternatively, use a simple SVG-to-PNG rasterizer (or just store raw SVG for HTML export)
4. For HTML export: render the raw SVG inline (no need for PNG)
5. For PDF/image export: a basic approach is to render SVG as a placeholder with its text content
6. Store the result in BlobStore and set po.BlobIdx

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/SVGObject.cs — SVG object with SvgData property

title: Render CheckBox in PDF export
description:
## Bug
export/pdf/export.go ExportBand switch — there is no case for preview.ObjectTypeCheckBox. Checkbox objects are silently skipped in PDF output (no rendering). The HTML and image exporters handle it correctly.

## Fix needed
1. Add 'case preview.ObjectTypeCheckBox:' in the PDF ExportBand switch
2. Draw a rectangle outline for the checkbox box
3. If obj.Text == 'true', draw an X or checkmark inside using PDF line operators
4. Follow the same pattern as the image exporter's checkbox rendering

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/Extras/Core/FastReport.Export.PdfSimple/ — PDF checkbox rendering

title: Fix AdvMatrix Serialize to write TableColumn/TableRow children
description:
## Bug
object/advmatrix.go:109-112 — Serialize() writes only DataSource and skips writing TableColumns and TableRows. The comment says 'the Writer interface does not currently expose arbitrary element writing'. This means AdvMatrix FRX round-trip loses the physical table layout.

## Fix needed
1. AdvMatrix Serialize should use WriteObjectNamed (now available on report.Writer) to write each TableColumn and TableRow as named child elements
2. Create serializable wrapper types (like matrix.go's headerHolder pattern) for AdvMatrixColumn, AdvMatrixRow, AdvMatrixCell
3. Write TableCell children within TableRow, and MatrixButton grandchildren within TableCell

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/AdvMatrix/ — AdvMatrixObject serialization

title: Fix ApplyStyle to propagate font changes from stylesheet
description:
## Bug
report/reportcomponent.go:150-153 — the ApplyStyle method has an empty block for FontChanged. Only FillColor and BorderColor are applied from style sheets. Font overrides (family, size, bold/italic) from named styles are silently ignored.

## Fix needed
1. In ApplyStyle(), when the style has a different font than default, propagate font changes to the target component
2. Use a type assertion to check if the component has SetFont() method
3. Apply Font.Name, Font.Size, Font.Style from the style to the component

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/ReportComponentBase.cs — ApplyStyle() method with full font propagation

title: Render Watermark in engine and exporters
description:
## Gap
ReportPage.Watermark is fully deserialized/serialized (reportpkg/watermark.go) but the engine never reads it or renders it on any page. All three exporters (HTML, image, PDF) skip it.

## What needs to happen
1. In engine/pages.go, when starting a new page, check currentPage.Watermark
2. If watermark has text: render as a large rotated text object behind other content
3. If watermark has an image: render as a background picture
4. Update export/pdf/export.go to emit watermark as a background content stream before page content
5. Update export/html/html.go to add watermark as a positioned div behind content
6. Update export/image/image.go to draw watermark on the canvas before bands

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Watermark.cs — Watermark properties
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Export/Html/HTMLExport.cs — HTML watermark rendering
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.Pages.cs — watermark placement during engine run

title: Fix ProcessAt deferred handlers to fire per-page instead of once
description:
## Bug
engine/processat.go:3 says 'simplified deferred-processing mechanism'. Deferred handlers fire only once per registration (one-shot). In the full FastReport engine, a TextObject with ProcessAt=PageFinished fires on EVERY page it appears on, not just once.

## Fix needed
1. Track which deferred handlers are per-occurrence vs per-page
2. For ProcessAt=PageFinished and ProcessAt=ColumnFinished, re-register the handler each time a page/column finishes
3. Only ProcessAt=ReportFinished should be one-shot

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.ProcessAt.cs — deferred handler dispatch with per-page re-firing

title: Implement KeepWithData engine logic
description:
## Gap
The C# engine has ReportEngine.KeepWithData.cs which ensures footer bands stay on the same page as the last data row. This file has no Go equivalent — engine/keep.go only handles the general keep-together mechanism.

## What needs to happen
1. Create engine/keepwithdata.go
2. When DataFooterBand.KeepWithData is true, check if the footer fits on the current page alongside the last data row
3. If not, move the last data row + footer to the next page
4. Also handle DataHeaderBand.KeepWithData (header stays with first data row)

## C# Reference
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.KeepWithData.cs — complete implementation
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/DataFooterBand.cs — KeepWithData property
- original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/DataHeaderBand.cs — KeepWithData property

title: Add missing standard functions: DateDiff, Format, StartsWith, EndsWith
description:
## Gap
functions/standard.go is mostly complete but missing several commonly-used FastReport functions:
- DateDiff() — difference between two dates in specified interval
- Format() — generic string formatting (Format('{0:C}', Price))
- StartsWith() / EndsWith() — string prefix/suffix checks
- Convert/casting functions

## What needs to happen
1. Add DateDiff(interval, date1, date2) — return difference in days/hours/minutes/seconds/months/years
2. Add Format(pattern, value) — handle .NET-style format patterns or Go fmt patterns
3. Add StartsWith(s, prefix) and EndsWith(s, suffix) boolean functions
4. Add ToInt(), ToFloat(), ToString() type conversion functions
5. Register all new functions in the standard function registry
6. Add unit tests for each function

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Functions/StdFunctions.cs` — Standard function registration
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Functions/DateTimeFunctions.cs` (if exists)
close_reason: Added DateDiff(interval, date1, date2), Format(pattern, value), StartsWith, EndsWith, ToInt, ToFloat, ToString. Registered all in All() map. Added unit tests.

title: SQL data source: implement query execution and row iteration
description:
## Gap
data/sql/connection.go only has DSN builders (PostgresConnection, MySQLConnection, etc.) but no query execution. There is no DataSource implementation that runs SQL queries and iterates results.

## What needs to happen
1. Add a SQLDataSource struct that embeds data.BaseDataSource
2. Implement Init() — connect to database using database/sql.Open() with the DSN from connection
3. Implement query execution — run the SQL query and store results
4. Populate columns from sql.Rows.Columns()
5. Implement First()/Next()/EOF()/GetValue() row iteration
6. Implement Close() to release database connection
7. Add RegisterSQLSource() helper to connect a report dictionary to a SQL database
8. Keep all drivers as optional imports (user imports the driver they need)

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Data/TableDataSource.cs` — TableDataSource with LoadData/Init
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Data/DataConnectionBase.cs` — Connection management
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Data/DataSourceBase.cs` — Base iteration
close_reason: data.TableDataSource in data/connection.go already implemented Init() with SQL execution and row loading into BaseDataSource. Added data/sql/datasource.go with NewSQLDataSource() and RegisterSQL() convenience helpers for wiring connections to dictionary

title: Barcode: implement actual encoding in Encode() methods
description:
## Gap
barcode/barcode.go has concrete barcode types (Code128Barcode, Code39Barcode, QRBarcode, etc.) but their Encode() methods are stubs that just store text without generating actual barcode data. No checksum calculation, no barcode bit-pattern generation, no image output.

## What needs to happen
1. Code128: implement encoding algorithm with start/stop characters, checksum calculation, pattern lookup table
2. Code39: implement character-to-bar mapping, optional checksum (CalcChecksum flag)
3. QR Code: implement QR matrix generation with error correction levels (or use existing Go QR library)
4. EAN-13/EAN-8: implement digit encoding with check digit validation
5. Each Encode() should produce barcode data that can be rendered as an image (bitmap or SVG)
6. Add a Render() or ToImage() method that converts encoded data to image.Image for BlobStore
7. Connect to engine/objects.go BarcodeObject case to generate actual barcode images

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Barcode/Barcode128.cs` — Code128 encoding
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Barcode/Barcode39.cs` — Code39 encoding
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Barcode/QRCode/` — QR code (13 files)

title: Engine: fix reprint headers type-switching
description:
## Gap
engine/reprint.go AddReprint() (line 30) has a comment 'A full implementation would type-switch on DataHeaderBand / GroupHeaderBand' but currently puts all bands into keepReprintFooters. Headers are never classified correctly, so they don't reprint at the top of new pages.

## What needs to happen
1. In AddReprint(), type-switch on *band.DataHeaderBand and *band.GroupHeaderBand to add to reprintHeaders list
2. Type-switch on *band.DataFooterBand and *band.GroupFooterBand to add to reprintFooters list
3. In ShowReprintHeaders(), render the stored header bands at the top of each new page
4. Call AddReprint() from engine rendering when DataHeader/GroupHeader bands with RepeatOnEveryPage=true are encountered
5. In startPage(), call ShowReprintHeaders() after showing PageHeader

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.Reprint.cs` — Reprint logic
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/HeaderFooterBandBase.cs` — RepeatOnEveryPage property
close_reason: AddReprint now correctly type-switches: added addReprintBand(isHeader), AddReprintDataHeader/Footer, AddReprintGroupHeader/Footer. DataHeaderBand/DataFooterBand with RepeatOnEveryPage=true now call AddReprint. startPage() calls ShowReprintHeaders() after PageHeader on every page.

title: Engine: implement keep-together band grouping
description:
## Gap
engine/keep.go has StartKeep()/EndKeep()/CheckKeepTogether()/FinishKeepTogether() but the engine never calls them. BandBase.KeepTogether=true is silently ignored — bands can split across pages even when they should stay together.

## What needs to happen
1. In engine/bands.go showFullBandOnce(), before rendering, check if the band has KeepTogether=true
2. If KeepTogether, call StartKeep() before rendering the band and its children
3. After rendering, call EndKeep() and CheckKeepTogether() to verify everything fits
4. If it doesn't fit, use CutObjects()/PasteObjects() to move bands to a new page
5. Handle the case where keep-together bands are larger than a full page (must break despite KeepTogether)
6. Integrate with DataBand rendering for groups that need keep-together

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.Keep.cs` — Keep-together logic
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.Bands.cs` — ShowBand with keep integration
close_reason: Implemented keep-together: CheckKeepTogether/FinishKeepTogether in startNewPageForCurrent, StartKeep/EndKeep in RunGroup and RunDataBandFull, nil guard in FinishKeepTogether

title: Engine: wire ProcessAt deferred text handling
description:
## Gap
engine/processat.go has AddDeferredHandler() and OnStateChanged() but the engine never uses them. TextObject.ProcessAt property is never checked during rendering in engine/objects.go. Text objects with ProcessAt=ReportFinished or DataFinished render immediately instead of being deferred.

## What needs to happen
1. In engine/objects.go buildPreparedObject, when handling TextObject, check ProcessAt property
2. If ProcessAt != Default, register a deferred handler via AddDeferredHandler() that evaluates the text later
3. Emit a placeholder PreparedObject with empty text for now
4. In engine lifecycle (OnStateChanged), when the matching state fires, evaluate the deferred text and update the PreparedObject
5. Map ProcessAt enum values to engine states: ReportFinished, DataFinished, PageFinished, ColumnFinished, GroupFinished
6. Call OnStateChanged at appropriate points in engine flow (end of data band, end of group, end of page, report finish)

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.ProcessAt.cs` — ProcessAt handling
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/TextObject.cs` — ProcessAt property
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Enums.cs` — ProcessAt enum
close_reason: Wired ProcessAt deferred text: populateBandObjects2 registers deferred handlers via AddDeferredHandler; OnStateChanged fired at endPage, endColumn, RunReportPage, runFinished, RunDataBandFull, RunGroup

title: Serialize Hyperlink properties on ReportComponentBase
description:
## Gap
ReportComponentBase.Serialize() in report/reportcomponent.go:273-314 does NOT write Hyperlink.* attributes. The Hyperlink struct may be populated but is lost on round-trip save.

## What needs to happen
1. In Serialize(), if rc.hyperlink is non-nil, write Hyperlink.Kind, Hyperlink.Expression, Hyperlink.Target, Hyperlink.Value
2. Add the missing Value field to the Hyperlink struct (currently only has Expression, Kind, Target)
3. This is the serialization counterpart to go-fastreport-dha (deserialization)

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Hyperlink.cs` — Hyperlink.Serialize()
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/ReportComponentBase.cs`
close_reason: Hyperlink.* attributes already serialized in Serialize() as part of dha implementation

title: Parameter expression evaluation at Prepare time
description:
## Gap
Parameters with Expression fields are deserialized from FRX but their expressions are never evaluated at Prepare time. In C#, parameters with expressions are computed before the engine starts rendering.

## What needs to happen
1. In engine/engine.go runPhase1 (or a new initParameters method), iterate all dictionary parameters
2. For each parameter with a non-empty Expression, call Report.Calc(expr) to evaluate it
3. Set the parameter's Value to the result
4. Handle nested parameters recursively (child params may reference parent params)
5. System variables should be initialized before parameter evaluation

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Data/Parameter.cs` — Parameter.CalcValue
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.cs` — RunPhase1 calls InitParameters
close_reason: Added initParameters() in engine.go: recursively evaluates Expression on all dictionary parameters via Report.Calc, called from runPhase1 after system vars and totals init

title: Resolve Dictionary Relations at Prepare time
description:
## Gap
data/helper.go Relation struct stores ParentSourceName/ChildSourceName as strings but ParentDataSource/ChildDataSource fields are never resolved from the dictionary. FindRelation() in helper.go compares DataSource interfaces but these are nil because they're never set. engine/relations.go works around this by doing its own lookup, but Dictionary-level resolution is needed for correctness.

## What needs to happen
1. Add a `ResolveRelations()` method to data.Dictionary
2. For each Relation, look up ParentSourceName and ChildSourceName via FindDataSourceByAlias()
3. Set ParentDataSource and ChildDataSource fields
4. Also resolve ParentColumns/ChildColumns from ParentColumnNames/ChildColumnNames
5. Call this in engine/engine.go runPhase1 after data sources are initialized
6. Update FindRelation to also match by source name as fallback

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Data/Relation.cs` — Relation.InitDataSource
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Data/Dictionary.cs` — RegisterRelation resolution
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.cs` — RunPhase1 data init
close_reason: Added Dictionary.ResolveRelations() in data/dictionary.go; updated FindRelation fallback by name in helper.go; called dict.ResolveRelations() from runPhase1 in engine.go

title: Render BarcodeObject in engine/objects.go
description:
## Gap
engine/objects.go buildPreparedObject has no case for *object.BarcodeObject. BarcodeObject is deserialized from FRX (registered in serial_registrations.go) but dropped during rendering.

## What needs to happen
1. Add `case *object.BarcodeObject:` to buildPreparedObject
2. Evaluate the barcode text expression via e.evalText(b.Text())
3. Use the barcode/ package encoders to generate an image (PNG bytes)
4. Store the image in BlobStore, set po.Kind = ObjectTypePicture and po.BlobIdx
5. Match barcode type name to the correct barcode/ sub-package encoder
6. Handle ShowText flag (render human-readable text below barcode)

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Barcode/BarcodeObject.cs` — BarcodeObject rendering and encoding
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Barcode/BarcodeBase.cs` — Base encoding
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Barcode/Barcode128.cs` — Code128 example

title: Render TableObject in engine/objects.go
description:
## Gap
engine/objects.go buildPreparedObject has no case for table.TableObject. TableObject is a complex component with rows, columns, and cells. In C# the engine has special handling to iterate table rows and render each cell as a separate object.

## What needs to happen
1. Add `case *table.TableObject:` (or *table.TableBase) to buildPreparedObject
2. TableObject has Rows/Columns/Cells; each TableCell is itself a container
3. Iterate all visible rows, for each row iterate visible columns
4. For each cell, compute the absolute position from column offsets + row offsets
5. Render cell content (text, pictures, etc.) as PreparedObjects with correct coordinates
6. Handle ColSpan/RowSpan (merged cells)
7. Handle cell borders and fills
8. Consider adding a special PreparedTable type to preview/ for structured output

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Table/TableObject.cs` — TableObject.Serialize/Draw/etc.
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Table/TableCell.cs` — Cell rendering
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Table/TableHelper.cs` — Layout computation
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.Bands.cs` — Engine table handling
close_reason: Added TableObject case in buildPreparedObject (bounding box as Shape); added populateTableObjects that computes colX/rowY offsets and renders each cell with ColSpan/RowSpan size as ObjectTypeText PreparedObject

title: Render ContainerObject children in engine/objects.go
description:
## Gap
engine/objects.go buildPreparedObject has no case for *object.ContainerObject. ContainerObject holds child objects inside it and should recursively render them with coordinate offsets.

## What needs to happen
1. Add `case *object.ContainerObject:` to buildPreparedObject switch
2. Set po.Kind to a container type (or ObjectTypeShape/picture as bounding box)
3. Recursively call buildPreparedObject for each child in c.Objects()
4. Offset child coordinates by the container's Left/Top position
5. Handle the container's Fill and Border
6. Consider clipping children that extend beyond the container bounds

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/ContainerObject.cs` — ContainerObject implementation
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Engine/ReportEngine.Bands.cs` — How engine handles containers
close_reason: Added ContainerObject case in buildPreparedObject (renders as Shape); added populateContainerChildren that recursively adds children with coordinate offsets; called from populateBandObjects2

title: Deserialize Hyperlink dot-notation attributes from FRX
description:
## Gap
ReportComponentBase.Deserialize() in report/reportcomponent.go:317-334 does NOT read Hyperlink.* dot-notation attributes (Hyperlink.Kind, Hyperlink.Expression, Hyperlink.Target). The Hyperlink struct exists at line 42 but is never populated during deserialization.

## What needs to happen
1. In ReportComponentBase.Deserialize(), read Hyperlink.Kind, Hyperlink.Expression, Hyperlink.Target (may also include Hyperlink.Value, Hyperlink.DetailPageName, Hyperlink.DetailReportName, Hyperlink.ReportParameter)
2. Construct a Hyperlink struct and assign to rc.hyperlink
3. In ReportComponentBase.Serialize(), write Hyperlink.* attributes when rc.hyperlink is non-nil and differs from defaults
4. Add Hyperlink.Value field to Hyperlink struct (currently only has Expression, Kind, Target)

## C# Reference
- `original-dotnet/FastReport.Base/Hyperlink.cs` — Full Hyperlink class with Serialize/Deserialize
